Kelly confirmed munitions situation 'shocking' on CBS: Tomahawks, ATACMS, SM-3s, THAAD, Patriot all deeply depleted. China's pre-summit posture: pressing Hormuz reopening (economic dependency) while endorsing Iranian 'legitimate right to peaceful use of nuclear energy' (Wang Yi May 6 baseline) — the structural contradiction that complicates MOU nuclear moratorium terms. CSIS: China 'confident enough to stand up to Trump on Iran.' CFR: Xi 'has shown little appetite to apply acute pressure on the regime.' Chinese firms confirmed selling satellite imagery used to target US forces. No new OFAC designations beyond Hengli/Qingdao Haiye.

sources.name: House of Commons Library briefing (updated 20h ago)

finding_cards.finding: The original 14-point MOU framework (12-15 year moratorium, HEU removal, IAEA snap inspections, no underground facilities, Hormuz phased reopening) is effectively dead. Iran's counter-proposal: decouple nuclear from war-ending; shorter enrichment moratorium; HEU to third country with return clause (no full surrender); no dismantlement. Trump rejected May 10 ('TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE'). Witkoff May 10 escalated to full dismantlement of Natanz/Fordow/Isfahan — abandoning original moratorium framework. 4th Oman round today (Day 74): 3+ hours, high-level only, no technical negotiators, 'difficult but constructive,' agree to continue. Talks have not formally collapsed but are now negotiating a meta-framework (framework-for-framework) rather than an MOU. Default mechanism (war-as-default-clause) not established. Fork D' (Hamas/Hezbollah deferral) probability increases as Iran's nuclear-deferral counter-proposal precisely mirrors that pattern.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: MOU formally collapses (principal-level rejection either side) — PARTIAL FIRE; 'Trump distances from Witkoff output' — INVERTED (Trump escalated beyond agents, not distanced from them)

discounts_applied: Single-source Witkoff dismantlement claim confirmed by multiple tier-2 outlets; discount not applied
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Oman talks continuing ('agree to continue') is the key counterweight — formal collapse not declared by either side. This is the ambiguity that keeps Fork D' elevated. The question is whether 'agree to continue' is a real negotiating process or face-saving before collapse. Trump saying he's 'still looking at it' on Saturday, then 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' Sunday, then talks continuing Monday is classic improvisational-principal oscillation (A1 validated).

Trump's Sunday 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' + Netanyahu call where Trump agreed HEU removal necessary + Witkoff's May 10 dismantlement demand (full facilities: Natanz/Fordow/Isfahan) = the original 12-15 year moratorium offer is no longer the US position. This is not the Feb 28 misrepresentation pattern (agents overstated Iranian flexibility); this is principal escalation that has dragged agents to a harder line. Iranian side: Araghchi (agent) continues diplomatic posture; Baqaei 'no nuclear negotiations' reflects coalition position; still no Mojtaba/Ghalibaf/Vahidi named endorsement of ANY deal terms. Iranian principal-level silence while agent engages = Hamas/Hezbollah deferral pattern confirmed active.

The Sunday night Netanyahu call is the most credible explanation for the hardening. This is not random oscillation; it's Netanyahu penetrating the US principal's decision process in real time.

Day 70 PM: IRGC Navy three-axis anti-ship attack demonstrated residual conventional capability. Iran 'lost track of mines' planted in Hormuz per Wikipedia/Hormuz crisis article — may indicate operational chaos or deliberate ambiguity to complicate US mine-clearing timeline. No new ISW capability assessment on post-strike inventory. Feigned-weakness hypothesis neither confirmed nor denied.

The return-clause demand specifically signals Iran does not consider its nuclear capacity 'spent.'

finding_cards.probe_name: Mutual Dispositional Reading and Power-Shift Perception

sources.name: Iran Kayhan editorial via Iran International (tier-2)

finding_cards.finding: Netanyahu's dispositional reading of Iran fully crystallized in CBS 60 Minutes: Iran 'committed to destroy America' (universal threat, not program-specific); enrichment 'sites must be dismantled'; proxies and ballistic missiles addressed before war can end; regime toppling 'possible'; Trump told Netanyahu 'I want to go in there.' This is the clearest articulation of the Powell shifting-power preventive logic yet: Netanyahu presenting Iran as dispositionally committed to regional destruction, not informationally deterrable. Iran's dispositional reading of US: Kayhan (Khamenei-supervised) 'Trump is not a passing phenomenon... mindset rooted in narcissism, delusions of superiority, threat-based tactics.' Baqaei's 'no nuclear negotiations' reflects belief that US will use any nuclear deal to continue coercing Iran. Per Weisiger: both sides in dispositional-reading mode → unlimited aims → MOU as tactical pause, not settlement.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: IMMEDIATE — BS-14: Netanyahu/coalition explicit 'regime nature' framing confirmed; 'IRGC explicit regime change is US objective' confirmed; Israeli unilateral strike probability elevated

variable_moved.driver: Netanyahu: 'if agreement... go in and take it out; if no agreement... won't say.' Powell preventive logic: Iran weakened now, window closing, preempt before regime recovers.

This is regime-change framing, not nuclear-program framing — confirms Weisiger unlimited-aims dynamic. However, Netanyahu also said 'if agreement... that's the best way' — leaving diplomatic door open. The spoiler risk is not certain; it's elevated.

proposed_revision.section: Section 8.1 Israeli unilateral strike probability / BS-14
proposed_revision.edit: Israeli unilateral strike within 14-21 day window revised to 20-30% (from 15-25%). Netanyahu's 60 Minutes CBS unlimited-aims framing + Trump agreement on HEU removal Sunday + Witkoff dismantlement = US-Israeli strategic convergence. The spoiler risk is now US-coordinated-or-unilateral, not purely unilateral — a structurally different and more dangerous variant. Fork B collapse mechanism: if Oman follow-up fails (likely given current gaps), coordinated US-Israeli action on HEU/facilities becomes the most probable next kinetic step.

trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: IMMEDIATE: MOU original framework dead; both principals expressed hard lines (PROBE-12-prime)

proposed_revision.edit: Add new structural dynamic: 'Meta-Negotiation Regression.' The 4th Oman round is no longer negotiating MOU terms — it is negotiating whether nuclear talks should be concurrent with or sequential to war-ending. This is a regression to the pre-war negotiating structure (see PBS timeline: May 11, 2025 = 4th round pre-war). The framework has returned to the pre-war bargaining structure despite 74 days of kinetics. Fearon-Slantchev mechanism implication: information rents from kinetics exhausted, but parties failed to coordinate on settlement before regressing to original impasse on sequencing. Fork D' (indefinite deferral) is now the default trajectory absent a forcing function.

Kelly (D-AZ) confirms munitions depletion 'shocking' on all major categories — this materially lowers Fork A sustainment ceiling.

proposed_revision.section: Section 3.3 Layer Three / Section 5.17 Cost Imposition
proposed_revision.edit: Goldman Sachs confirms 101→98 days global stocks by end May; product scarcity onset 4-6 weeks in South Africa, India, Thailand, Taiwan. Both-sides strangulation dual-compression (introduced Day 72) remains operative but is now being price-discovered by markets. Mine-tracking loss by Iran = structural impediment to Hormuz reopening independent of political deal (new finding — add to Section 5).

calibration_check.reasoning: 8 fired (3 IMMEDIATE triggers), 3 partial, 1 null, 0 gap across 13 probes. The aggregate finding: both principals have now expressed real red lines (Trump/Witkoff: dismantlement; Iran: nuclear-deferral) that are structurally incompatible with the original MOU 14-point framework. The PA-gap has inverted on the US side (principal more hawkish than agents). Both sides are in Weisiger dispositional-reading mode. Oman talks continue ('agree to continue') but on meta-negotiation footing, not MOU terms. This is more than drift — the original MOU framework is dead. What continues is the search for a new framework. The framework thesis (constraint architecture precedes faction decisions) HOLDS: time arithmetic, military physics, cost arithmetic still bind. But the Fearon-Slantchev bargaining mechanism has stalled at the coordination stage: information rents exhausted, but parties cannot coordinate on focal point because Schelling focal points (May 8-9, May 11 Oman, May 14-15 Trump-Xi) are being consumed without producing convergence. The framework requires synthesis v3.1 if: (a) Trump-Xi fails to produce Iranian movement on nuclear, or (b) Israeli unilateral/coordinated strike occurs in the 14-21 day window.
calibration_check.synthesis_revision_recommendation: Hold v3.0 pending Trump-Xi May 14-15 outcome. Three immediate triggers filed. If Trump-Xi produces no Iranian concession on nuclear sequencing AND Israeli action does not occur, initiate v3.1 with: (1) Fork B revised to 20-28%; (2) Fork D' elevated to 20-25%; (3) Meta-Negotiation Regression as new structural mechanism; (4) PA-gap inversion as Layer 5 update; (5) THREE-CSG force posture correction; (6) Mine-tracking structural impediment addition.
